label: DevSecOps Engineer
description: Integrates security into software delivery pipelines — SAST, DAST, SCA, container security, IaC security, and secrets management embedded in CI/CD workflows.

paths.name: Practical DevSecOps Track
paths.rationale: CDP is the de facto DevSecOps standard. CDE adds advanced automation depth. GCSA provides GIAC vendor-neutral cloud security automation recognition alongside the Practical DevSecOps stack.
pros: CDP is the most recognized DevSecOps credential in the practitioner community
GCSA adds GIAC recognition alongside CDP practitioner credibility
Strong combination for DevSecOps engineering roles at mature organizations
cons: Requires actual DevOps engineering experience to be effective

knowledge: CI/CD concepts (GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, or Jenkins)
prerequisites.experience: Software development or DevOps engineering background strongly recommended.

cert_sequence.notes: Practical DevSecOps flagship cert. SAST, DAST, SCA tool integration into CI/CD, container security, IaC security, compliance-as-code. 6-hour practical exam with 5 challenges plus report. Requires basic Linux and scripting proficiency.

Certified DevSecOps Expert — requires CDP. Custom SAST rule development, advanced DAST automation, RASP/IAST, secrets management with HashiCorp Vault. 24-hour practical exam — the most demanding in the Practical DevSecOps portfolio.
GIAC Cloud Security Automation — DevSecOps in cloud environments, IaC security, container security automation. Adds GIAC recognition that CDP lacks. Associated with SANS SEC540.

paths.name: Supply Chain Security Track
paths.rationale: For DevSecOps engineers focused on software supply chain security — SBOM, SLSA framework, dependency security, and code signing. Fast-growing specialization driven by regulatory pressure.
pros: CSSE is currently the only dedicated supply chain security cert
EO 14028 and NIST SSDF creating regulatory demand for this expertise
CSSE + CDP gives the broadest DevSecOps credential coverage
cons: CSSE very new — employer recognition still developing
Supply chain security requires understanding the full software development lifecycle

cert_sequence.notes: Foundation DevSecOps credential. The pipeline security foundation that supply chain work builds on.

Certified Software Supply Chain Security Expert — SLSA framework, SBOM generation and analysis, dependency security, code signing, cloud supply chain attacks. 6-hour practical exam. One of the only dedicated credentials in this space.

Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist — container and K8s security bridges DevSecOps and supply chain through image security, registry scanning, and runtime security. Prerequisite: active CKA certification.
